text.title: Deploy Scripts for Solo VPS Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Deploy Scripts for Solo VPS` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Deploy MUST rsync into a staging directory then atomic-switch via `mv`. Direct rsync into runtime dir = REJECT.
rule.rationale: rsync over a live runtime leaves half-old / half-new files; atomic mv makes the switch instantaneous and reversible.

rule.statement: Deploy MUST run lint + tests on the source before rsync. Failing tests = REJECT (exit non-zero).
rule.rationale: Shipping known-broken code is the most expensive way to find a bug; a 30s lint+test gate saves a 30-minute rollback.

rule.statement: Deploy MUST call the health endpoint after systemd reload and require HTTP 200 within 30s. No-smoke deploy = REJECT.
rule.rationale: systemd reload returns before the new process is actually serving; smoke check is the only honest signal.

rule.statement: Deploy MUST keep `current` symlink pointing at the new release dir and `previous` symlink at the prior one. Rollback = one mv. Missing symlink scheme = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Without the symlink scheme, rollback means re-rsyncing the old tag — slow and error-prone.

rule.statement: Every step MUST propagate exit codes; `set -euo pipefail` mandatory. Background tasks must `wait`. Missing propagation = REJECT.
rule.rationale: A failed pip install in step 3 must abort step 4 (systemd reload); without propagation the script ships broken code and exits 0.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
